channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photo shows U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler wearing blackface at college Halloween party in 2006
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — U.S. Rep. Mike Lawler said he was sorry Thursday after the New York Times obtained photos of him wearing blackface about two decades ago at a college Halloween costume party where he dressed as Michael Jackson.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Obie Williams could hear babies crying and branches battering the windows when he answered his daughter’s daily phone call last week as Hurricane Helene tore through her rural Georgia town.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Colorado county clerk Tina Peters sentenced to 9 years for voting data scheme
item.description: A judge ripped into a Colorado county clerk for her crimes and lies before sentencing her Thursday to nine years behind bars for a data-breach scheme spawned from the rampant false claims about voting machine fraud in the 2020 presidential race.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania U.S. Senate Debate: Bob Casey, Dave McCormick appear for first debate
item.description: It's Debate Night in Pennsylvania with Bob Casey and Dave McCormick going head-to-head for the first time.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign after the issue lingered on the margins for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blue Ridge Parkway to be closed indefinitely, National Park Service says
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona man sentenced after setting fire to Grand Canyon National Park cabin where ex-girlfriend stayed
item.description: A man who set fire to a cabin inside the Grand Canyon National Park was sentenced to 20 months in federal prison, authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, Fox 5

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Woman recounts her rescue from river, husband's death
item.description: WASHINGTON COUNTY, Tenn. (WJHL) — Some of the best years of Vicki Hunter's life came to a tragic end Friday after a raging Nolichucky River ripped through the bottomland where she and her husband, Jerry, lived with more than a dozen horses.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Irate residents halt construction of migrant shelter
item.description: Residents of a southeast Juarez neighborhood are demanding the government call off plans to build a migrant shelter near their children’s schools.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida communities hit three times by hurricanes grapple with how and whether to rebuild
item.description: HORSESHOE BEACH, Fla. (AP) — It was just a month ago that Brooke Hiers left the state-issued emergency trailer where her family had lived since Hurricane Idalia slammed into her Gulf Coast fishing village of Horseshoe Beach in August 2023.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID R. MARTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Valadao, Salas spar over issues in California debate
item.description: David Valadao and Rudy Salas debated over issues voters said are of the most importance to them during a debate.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: Explainer: How immigrants end up on ICE's non-detained docket
item.description: Recent reports that over 435,000 convicted criminals are on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement's non-detained docket (NDD) -- meaning they are not detained by the agency while they await immigration proceedings -- has spurred much criticism and confusion.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Missing Missouri mother's remains found 6 months after mysterious disappearance
item.description: Authorities in Missouri have identified the skeletal remains of mother Emily Strite who was found in a remote wooded area six months after she mysteriously disappeared.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Illegal immigrants with 'terrorism ties' will continue to exploit border, Homeland Security report warns
item.description: A Department of Homeland Security threat assessment report released Wednesday warned of threats to the border, the election process and migration flows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump on Springfield Haitian migrants: 'They have to be removed'
item.description: The former president spoke to NewsNation's Ali Bradley about his plans regarding the Haitian migrants living in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee state authorities said Wednesday they were investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon is convicted of threatening former Speaker McCarthy
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man was convicted Wednesday of threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy after becoming upset that the government had not shot down a Chinese spy balloon that floated over his home city.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: After employees swept away in Helene flooding, Tennessee factory under investigation
item.description: Several employees have accused the company of not letting them leave soon enough as flooding set in on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, Fox 5

item.title: Melania Trump in new memoir calls abortion a 'woman's fundamental right': Report
item.description: “Why should anyone other than the woman herself have the power to determine what she does with her own body?" asks the former first lady.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Grandparents found hugging one another after fallen tree killed them in their South Carolina home
item.description: As Hurricane Helene roared outside, the wind howling and branches snapping, John Savage went to his grandparents' bedroom to make sure they were OK.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Oasis adds US, Canada and Mexico stops to reunion tour
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Oasis shocked fans by announcing a reunion tour last month, ending a 15-year hiatus and, presumably, the long-held feud between brothers Liam and Noel Gallagher. But those were only U.K. dates. On Monday, the band shared that they're planning on heading to North America in 2025, too.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chevy and GMC truck owners could get up to $12,700 as part of $35M settlement
item.description: Owners of the affected trucks could get anywhere from $400 to $12,712. Here's how to tell if you qualify.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5

item.title: Fat Bear Week postponed due to bear vs. bear violence
item.description: Fat Bear Week is off to a violent start this year. The bracket and all the chunky contestants were scheduled to be revealed on Monday morning, but the reveal was pushed back by a day after one bear, No. 469, attacked and killed another bear, No. 402.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Frankie Valli addresses fan concerns about elder abuse
item.description: Legendary pop singer Frankie Valli says his choice to continue performing into his 90s is his own, despite concerns about possible elder abuse that stem from viral videos of the singer's recent shows.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Pilot flying Helene rescue missions in NC ordered out, threatened with arrest
item.description: The fire official told him to tell any other pilots he knew they would also be arrested if they came back.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, KRON4

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Cabrel Ngounou's life in Cameroon quickly unraveled after neighbors caught the teenager with his boyfriend. A crowd surrounded his boyfriend's house and beat him. Ngounou's family learned of the relationship and kicked him out.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and TERRY CHEA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Leslie forms; Hurricane Kirk strengthens
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to grow larger as it strengthens in the Atlantic, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC
